papers.title: Spatial Priming Outperforms Semantic Prompting: A Grid-Based Approach to Improving LLM Accuracy on Chart Data Extraction
papers.innovation: Proposes a grid-based spatial priming approach that significantly improves LLM accuracy in extracting data from scientific charts, outperforming traditional semantic prompting methods. This directly addresses a critical, practical challenge in data analysis.
papers.benchmarks: Improved accuracy (e.g., precision, recall, F1-score) on extracting data points from non-standardized scientific charts, compared to existing prompting strategies.
papers.use_case: Automated data extraction from complex scientific charts for large-scale literature analysis, research data aggregation, and augmenting data analytics platforms with visual data parsing capabilities.

papers.title: SkillLens: Adaptive Multi-Granularity Skill Reuse for Cost-Efficient LLM Agents
papers.innovation: Introduces SkillLens, an adaptive framework that allows LLM agents to reuse procedural experiences at multiple granularities. This innovation optimizes for cost-efficiency by injecting relevant skills without introducing unnecessary details, balancing performance and token usage.
papers.benchmarks: Demonstrates improvements in cost-efficiency (e.g., reduced token count or API calls) while maintaining or improving task performance (e.g., success rate, accuracy) for LLM agents across various complex tasks requiring skill reuse.
papers.use_case: Designing and deploying cost-effective and performant LLM agents in environments where API costs or inference latency are critical. Applicable to agents that need to leverage a wide range of skills without incurring excessive operational expenses.

papers.title: CoCoDA: Co-evolving Compositional DAG for Tool-Augmented Agents
papers.innovation: Presents CoCoDA, a framework that enables the co-evolution of an agent's planner and its tool library using a compositional Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG). This addresses the significant challenge of scaling tool-augmented agents by efficiently managing an expanding set of external executable skills.
papers.benchmarks: Evaluated on the efficiency of tool retrieval, scalability of tool libraries, and overall performance of tool-augmented LLM agents in complex, multi-step tasks, demonstrating how to maintain performance within fixed computational constraints.
papers.use_case: Developing scalable and robust tool-augmented LLM agents that can integrate and evolve with large, dynamic libraries of external tools (APIs, functions, databases). Essential for building general-purpose agents capable of solving a wide array of real-world problems.
